pub struct Partition { pub buckets: HashMap<Address, Bucket>, pub unbucketed: Vec<(usize, Recovered<TransactionSigned>)> }
impl Partition { pub fn sort_all_parallel(mut self) -> Self { ... } pub fn verify_all_parallel(&self) -> Result<(), SequencingRightsError> { ... } pub fn reassemble(self, total_count: usize) -> Vec<Recovered<TransactionSigned>> { ... } }
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds partition management for bucketed and unbucketed transactions, parallel bucket sort/verify, and consensus-critical reassembly that preserves original inter-bucket positions and panics if any transaction position is lost.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact preservation keeps transaction-loss guards and deterministic reassembly behavior unchanged.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A partition reassembly with a missing position should panic instead of silently dropping a transaction.

pub struct BucketManager;
impl BucketManager { pub fn create_partition<S: StateProvider>(&mut self, classifications: Vec<(usize, Recovered<TransactionSigned>, Classification)>, reader: &SequencingRightsReader, state: &S) -> Partition { ... } }
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds `BucketManager` partitioning from classified transactions, using `SequencingRightsReader` to bucket single sequencing-rights contracts and leaving multi-contract, no-rights, simple-transfer, exempt, or failed classifications unbucketed.
